story_model.delivery_policy: complete-first: every in-scope capability CAP-001..CAP-013 and acceptance contract AC-001..AC-016 ships in this feature; phases order dependencies and never create an MVP, P0, v2, or future-work reduction
story_model.test_first_policy: For each behavior boundary, land the named contract/refusal/fault test task before its implementation task. Later real-entrypoint evidence tasks validate integrated artifacts and do not substitute for test-first contract work.

phases.purpose: Freeze regressions, portable fixtures, pinned tooling, and isolated evidence roots before architectural changes.
phases.completion_gate: Baseline receipts and reusable disposable fixtures exist; dependencies are exact and no runtime behavior has changed.

phases.purpose: Establish contracts, Kernel gates, journal, bounded queries, and CLI compatibility required by every story.
phases.completion_gate: The shared Kernel boundary and data contracts are executable and tested before public lifecycle/MCP work.

phases.purpose: Deliver safe configured-project lifecycle and bounded observation through backward-compatible CLI and real stdio MCP.
phases.story: As a coding agent, I can inspect and safely run, start, stop, restart, or clean a configured low-risk local task through one control plane, with an evidence-backed result and no unsafe raw controls.
phases.completion_gate: All 18 tools are real-wire callable, low-risk lifecycle works, refusals are enforced, CLI schemaVersion 1 remains compatible, and adoption inspection proves zero writes.

phases.purpose: Deliver lost-response recovery, no-replay semantics, and standalone CLI takeover after adapter/runtime loss.
phases.story: As a developer, I can recover or reconcile uncertain Agent work and take over a still-running service from the CLI without duplication or guessing effects.
phases.completion_gate: Known/lost operation IDs, same-ID/digest rules, indeterminate effects, cross-surface recovery, and adapter-exit takeover pass deterministic fault tests.

phases.purpose: Deliver canonical Skill routing, non-destructive installation diagnosis, one compatibility identity, and two generated host artifacts.
phases.story: As a Codex or Claude user, I can install the canonical Skill and a self-contained host Plugin whose routing and shared-state behavior do not drift or overwrite my divergent content.
phases.completion_gate: Skill traces, duplicate-copy matrix, compatibility manifest, isolated runtime, and separate policy-free Codex/Claude packages pass.

phases.purpose: Produce exact real-entrypoint host and OS receipts and withhold every unproven readiness claim.
phases.story: As a release consumer, I can see exactly which CLI, MCP, Plugin, host-version, lifecycle, recovery, and OS cells were actually proven.
phases.completion_gate: Windows/macOS/Linux core and bundle cells plus separate real Codex/Claude and cross-host lifecycle cells are recorded as passed, failed, blocked, unsupported, or stale without roll-up.

phases.purpose: Audit exclusions, packaging, help/docs, complete regression, and evidence-to-claim traceability.
phases.completion_gate: No deferred/dropped surface leaked, all baseline/package/security checks pass, and every release statement resolves to exact evidence cells.

story_model.parallelism_rule: Tasks may run in parallel only when their dependencies are complete and expected write scopes do not overlap; host/OS evidence cells are intentionally separable after one immutable build identity exists.
story_model.external_evidence_rule: A missing real host/OS runner marks only that evidence cell blocked and withholds its claim; it does not permit a synthetic pass or reduce the implementation scope.

task_candidates.objective: Freeze the current CLI/control-plane/package behavior and create the disposable cross-platform lifecycle fixture used by every later contract and evidence lane.
verification: fixture proves low-risk managed/finite, medium-risk, safe-clean, protected evidence, verified/unknown/external/stale ownership cases
task_candidates.done_condition: Baseline outputs/exit codes and fixture digest are reproducible under isolated LAUNCHDECK_HOME without touching unrelated processes or paths.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop and reopen if the fixture requires a second lifecycle backend or cannot avoid non-temporary processes/clean targets.

task_candidates.objective: Pin the planned MCP SDK v1.x, compatible Zod, esbuild, and node-semver toolchain and create inert source/evidence directory boundaries without implementing behavior.
task_candidates.done_condition: The repository installs deterministically on Node >=20 and no shipped Plugin runtime behavior or readiness is claimed yet.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop and reopen if the selected SDK/toolchain cannot produce a relocatable single-file ESM runtime without Plugin-local node_modules/global CLI/npx/install scripts.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing contract tests for the exact 18-operation registry, closed input catalog, nine-field Agent result, independent compatibility axes, canonical digests, and all deferred/dropped exclusions.
verification: tests fail before implementation for missing catalog/validators
task_candidates.done_condition: Tests encode the complete public/excluded contract without granting an execution route to deferred or dropped capabilities.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if any required public operation or exact result field conflicts with the approved schemas, or if a deferred operation becomes required.

task_candidates.objective: Implement the frozen Operation Registry, schema validators, canonical input/component digests, AgentOperationResult normalizer, and compatibility manifest primitives until FND-001 passes.
verification: registry has exactly 18 Agent-exposed entries and no state/effect/transport code
task_candidates.done_condition: FND-001 is green and every later adapter can consume one frozen catalog/result/compatibility contract.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if the registry becomes an execution/state authority or if CLI schemaVersion and Agent protocol must be conflated.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing Kernel pipeline tests for deterministic project resolution, trusted/untrusted context, dynamic risk, ownership, compatibility, lock ordering, Plugin-cwd refusal, and mutation revalidation.
verification: medium/high/destructive public mutations refuse despite permissive annotations
sync/async mutation lock paths expose one order
task_candidates.done_condition: Tests prove adapters cannot supply policy-sensitive context or bypass Kernel gates.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if a host requires Plugin install cwd as project authority, an adapter must decide risk/ownership, or lock convergence cannot preserve current resource truth.

task_candidates.objective: Implement Application Kernel query/execute/recover scaffolding, project-context resolver, policy/compatibility pipeline, and one ordered mutation-lock authority over existing control-plane truth.
task_candidates.done_condition: FND-003 passes, Kernel is the only planned mutation entry, and existing registry/run/process/ownership stores remain authoritative.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if implementation needs a second registry/run backend, adapter-side authorization, or a long journal lock across OS effects.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing journal tests for prepared-before-effects, atomic transitions, same-ID/digest recovery, mismatch/missing refusal, index rebuild, 30-day terminal retention, unresolved retention, crash windows, and no replay.
verification: deterministic faults before journal, after prepared, after running, after effect, after terminal, before response
newer schema blocks writes but permits bounded diagnosis
corrupt/missing index rebuilds only from records
task_candidates.done_condition: The failure-first suite proves journal evidence cannot become a second run truth and every uncertain path stops or reconciles.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if durable prepared cannot precede effects, unresolved records must age-prune, or recovery requires invoking the original handler.

task_candidates.objective: Implement the versioned per-record operation journal, atomic result blobs, rebuildable creation index, events, retention/compaction, and Kernel transition integration until FND-005 passes.
task_candidates.done_condition: FND-005 passes; prepared is durable before effects, unresolved evidence is retained, and list/get/reconcile storage primitives never execute work.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop on non-atomic records, an authoritative correlation index, replay-capable reconciliation, or journal/resource truth duplication.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing bounded-query tests for capabilities, diagnosis, project/task inspection, logs/events cursors, exact operation correlation bounds, and zero-write adoption inspection.
verification: no arbitrary path or permanent follow
operation.list <=15 minutes and <=20 with zero/unique/ambiguous results
before/after inventory proves adoption.inspect has no config/registry/journal/run/process/port writes
task_candidates.done_condition: All query boundaries and the adoption negative contract are encoded before handlers exist.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if adoption requires writes/start, observations require arbitrary paths/follow, or public operation search must become unbounded.

task_candidates.objective: Implement capabilities/diagnosis/project/task query handlers, stable bounded observation cursors, operation correlation/get primitives, and read-only adoption inspection until FND-007 passes.
task_candidates.done_condition: FND-007 passes with bounded/redacted results, exact compatibility/provenance, and adoption.inspect that creates no journal record or apply authorization.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if a query mutates shared state, cursor tokens select arbitrary data, or Plugin cwd becomes a target default.

task_candidates.objective: Expand CLI regression tests to require schemaVersion 1 text/JSON/compact/exit compatibility and a nested normalized Agent result for migrated query routes before CLI extraction.
verification: golden semantic fields and exit codes for success/failure/partial
compact output retains current field projection
Agent result does not replace legacy envelope
CLI-only routes remain reachable but absent from Agent operations
task_candidates.done_condition: Tests fail only on missing Kernel routing/nested Agent semantics and preserve every existing public CLI meaning.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if outcome/resource separation cannot coexist with schemaVersion 1 without breaking existing automation.

task_candidates.objective: Create the declarative CLI operation map and cut capabilities/diagnosis/bounded query/adoption-preview routes over to the Kernel while preserving legacy rendering and CLI-only behavior.
task_candidates.done_condition: FND-009 passes and query/adoption CLI routes consume Kernel results without reconstructing safety or changing existing output meanings.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if cli.js retains an independent policy/mutation implementation for a migrated route or if compact/text behavior breaks.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing shared lifecycle cases for configured low-risk task.start/stop/restart/run, concurrent/repeated start reuse, verified ownership, medium-plus refusal, partial restart, and forbidden raw inputs.
verification: one spawn receipt under sequential/concurrent repeat
medium/high/destructive and raw controls refuse before effects
restart uses one operation identity and represents partial state honestly
task_candidates.done_condition: The lifecycle contract is executable against the Kernel and fails before handler migration.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if public Agent lifecycle needs force/external/medium-plus execution or duplicate-start safety cannot be preserved.

task_candidates.objective: Implement Kernel task lifecycle handlers and migrate safe CLI start/dev/run/aliases/stop/restart routes while retaining force/risky/follow behavior as CLI-only.
task_candidates.done_condition: US1-001 passes and all migrated safe lifecycle routes execute once through Kernel with current ownership/risk/lock checks.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if any migrated adapter mutates outside Kernel, or if preserving existing CLI requires exposing broader behavior to Agent surfaces.

task_candidates.objective: Write failing safe-clean contract and fault cases for canonical plans, protected evidence, symlink/outside/root refusal, digest drift, pre-delete refusal, positive apply, and reconciliation evidence.
verification: old digest returns plan_digest_mismatch, effects none, changed=false, no deletion
fresh digest deletes only safe current targets
no risky target/yes/confirmed input in public schema
task_candidates.done_condition: The clean behavior and exclusion boundary fail before the Kernel clean implementation exists.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if safe apply cannot recompute under locks or any public Agent route needs risky clean/model-authored confirmation.

task_candidates.objective: Implement clean.plan and journaled clean.applySafe with canonical digest/revalidation and map legacy CLI dry-run/safe behavior without exposing risky clean.
task_candidates.done_condition: US1-003 passes; every deletion is safe-plan-bound and legacy CLI behavior remains schemaVersion 1 compatible.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if clean requires caller paths, risky targets, model-authored confirmation, service stop, or deletion of shared recovery evidence.

task_candidates.objective: Build the real stdio client suite first: initialize/list/call, all 18 schemas, structured results, protocol/tool/domain errors, stdout purity, child-output isolation, interruption/restart, concurrent IDs, and Plugin-cwd scope refusal.
verification: spawn shipped/server entrypoint through official SDK client transport
server exit leaves managed run alive
task_candidates.done_condition: The real-wire suite fails on the absent MCP entrypoint and encodes no direct-handler shortcut.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if the official SDK cannot meet framing/lifecycle requirements or stdio transport requires Plugin-local state/CLI subprocess orchestration.

task_candidates.objective: Implement the SDK-based local stdio server as a pure projection of the 18 registry entries with trusted context separation, serialized stdout, bounded stderr, and normalized structuredContent.
task_candidates.done_condition: US1-005 passes over the real process boundary and MCP shutdown owns no managed-task lifetime.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if any MCP handler owns policy/state/effects, if stdout cannot remain pure, or if a second backend is required.

task_candidates.objective: Run CLI/MCP normalized-equivalence, cross-surface start reuse, public refusal, safe-clean drift, and adoption zero-write journeys through actual CLI and stdio entrypoints.
verification: compare semantic fields, not raw host formatting
state/process/port inventories prove no forbidden effects
task_candidates.done_condition: Every US1 quickstart journey passes through real entrypoints with one Kernel result authority and no unsupported readiness claim.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen on irreconcilable CLI schemaVersion 1 semantic mismatch, any forbidden effect, or adoption write/start.

task_candidates.objective: Resolve only defects exposed by US1-007 across Kernel/CLI/MCP projections and freeze semantic-equivalence fixtures without broadening the public surface.
task_candidates.done_condition: US1-007 and baseline suites pass; expected fixtures normalize only IDs/timestamps and allowed provenance differences.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop rather than weaken fixtures if parity would require a second authority, unsafe input, schemaVersion break, or deferred capability.

task_candidates.objective: Write end-to-end response-loss and crash tests across CLI/MCP for known ID, lost-ID bounded correlation, zero/unique/ambiguous candidates, same/different digest, missing/expired records, restart, and indeterminate foreground work.
verification: real CLI and stdio process boundaries
external handler/effect counter never exceeds one
no recovery branch creates a prepared record for supplied recovery ID
task_candidates.done_condition: The suite fails on incomplete public recovery routes but proves every forbidden retry/replay path.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if transport uncertainty cannot be represented without retry or if exactly-once language is required.

task_candidates.objective: Complete operation.list/get/reconcile Kernel semantics, CLI recovery routes, MCP recovery projection, and Skill-neutral recovery metadata until US2-001 passes without invoking original handlers.
task_candidates.done_condition: US2-001 passes and all recovery routes return/reconcile original evidence or stop, never re-execute.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if reconcile must call the original handler, correlation becomes broad search, or missing/digest-mismatch IDs can create work.

task_candidates.objective: Test that MCP/adapter exit and compatible runtime replacement preserve one managed run and shared state, and that standalone CLI capabilities/inspect/logs/stop take over using the same run/PID/port/state home.
verification: spawn MCP entrypoint, start, terminate adapter, assert process/state alive
compatible runtime observes identical IDs and stops only after compatibility/ownership proof
incompatible runtime diagnoses and refuses writes
task_candidates.done_condition: The real lifecycle suite records unchanged state/service identity across adapter exit/replacement and safe CLI takeover.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if adapter lifetime owns a managed process, component mismatch can write, or compatible versions cannot share the existing state authority.

task_candidates.objective: Resolve takeover/compatibility gaps exposed by US2-003 and finalize capabilities provenance/write gates without adding Plugin-local state or process ownership.
task_candidates.done_condition: US2-003 passes; diagnosis remains available where safe, incompatible writes fail closed, and compatible takeover never respawns or loses evidence.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if fixes require state migration on adapter/package lifecycle or a separate Plugin state namespace.

task_candidates.objective: Write ordered Skill trace evaluations for intent gate, capabilities, observe-before-mutate, MCP-first selection, pre-dispatch safe CLI fallback, business-refusal stop, uncertainty recovery, and every forbidden intent/call.
verification: assert actual call order, not final prose
fallback only before any mutation call sent
task_candidates.done_condition: Evaluation fixtures fail against the current CLI-only Skill and encode the complete routing/refusal state machine.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if a target host cannot expose capability/transport state needed to distinguish safe pre-dispatch fallback from uncertainty.

task_candidates.objective: Update the one canonical launchdeck-agent Skill and references to the validated MCP-first/fallback/refusal/recovery state machine and remove public Agent routing to force/risky/follow paths.
verification: canonical references contain no public force-stop/risky/permanent-follow route
task_candidates.done_condition: US3-001 passes and one reference-driven Skill produces safe deterministic sequences across healthy, unavailable, refused, and uncertain states.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if implementation requires competing Skills, model-authored policy/approval, or fallback after possible dispatch.

task_candidates.objective: Write installer/doctor tests for current, legacy, project, and Plugin Skill copies; canonical content digests; identical precedence; divergent preservation; idempotent current-path install; and no automatic overwrite/delete.
verification: preferred $HOME/.agents path for new Codex install
divergent bytes unchanged after doctor/install/update attempts
force cannot silently overwrite divergent user content
task_candidates.done_condition: The non-destructive compatibility matrix fails against current installer behavior and names deterministic guidance.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if host discovery requires silent winner selection or automatic modification of divergent user content.

task_candidates.objective: Implement canonical Skill content manifests and non-destructive current/legacy/project/Plugin diagnosis/install behavior until US3-003 passes.
task_candidates.done_condition: US3-003 passes; all copies remain observable, identical precedence is deterministic, and divergent content is preserved and write-blocking.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if current-path migration requires deleting/overwriting legacy or divergent content automatically.

task_candidates.objective: Write deterministic build, manifest-policy, archive inventory, component digest, no-external-import, relocation, Node prerequisite, and isolated real-stdio tests for separate Codex and Claude artifacts.
verification: two clean builds are byte/tree deterministic except declared metadata
esbuild metafile has no external npm imports
isolated paths include spaces/non-ASCII and no node_modules/global CLI/npx/install scripts
host templates contain no operation/risk/state/recovery policy
task_candidates.done_condition: The build/isolation suite fails before generator/templates exist and distinguishes generation proof from host readiness.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if a host artifact requires install-time code, global CLI, unresolved npm import, native binary, or policy-bearing manifest.

task_candidates.objective: Implement one compatibility/build identity, single-file ESM runtime build, policy-free Codex/Claude renderers, deterministic archives/integrity sidecars, and package scripts until US3-005 passes.
verification: launch each isolated runtime through real MCP client
task_candidates.done_condition: US3-005 passes; artifacts are separate, relocatable, self-contained, identity-bound, and contain the same canonical Skill without owning state/service lifetime.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Stop if generation cannot remain deterministic/policy-free or artifacts need a different runtime/state authority.

task_candidates.objective: Implement the exact ReadinessEvidenceCell schema/runner that records build, surface, operations, host/version/scope, OS/arch, scenario, result, raw refs, and invalidation without roll-up.
verification: schema rejects missing dimensions and unsupported inferred rollups
evidence points to immutable build identity/raw refs
task_candidates.done_condition: Evidence cells can represent every required matrix dimension and cannot turn a missing cell into readiness.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if release tooling requires a package-wide boolean that cannot preserve exact evidence dimensions.

task_candidates.objective: Execute and record Windows core CLI, real stdio, isolated bundle, fault/recovery, and adapter-exit takeover cells for the immutable candidate build.
verification: run candidate CLI/stdio/bundle from disposable Windows fixture
record raw stdout/stderr/state/process receipts and build/OS/Node identity
task_candidates.done_condition: Every required Windows core/stdio/bundle cell has an honest terminal evidence state and only passing cells support claims.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen only if Windows behavior contradicts architecture/scope; otherwise mark environmental absence/failure on the exact cell and route defects to owning task.

task_candidates.objective: Execute and record macOS core CLI, real stdio, isolated bundle, fault/recovery, and adapter-exit takeover cells for the same immutable build.
verification: run candidate CLI/stdio/bundle from disposable macOS fixture
do not import Windows pass status
task_candidates.done_condition: Every required macOS core/stdio/bundle cell has an honest terminal evidence state and only passing cells support claims.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen only on architecture/scope contradiction; otherwise preserve failed/blocked/unsupported cells without cross-OS inference.

task_candidates.objective: Execute and record Linux core CLI, real stdio, isolated bundle, fault/recovery, and adapter-exit takeover cells for the same immutable build.
verification: run candidate CLI/stdio/bundle from disposable Linux fixture
do not import Windows/macOS pass status
task_candidates.done_condition: Every required Linux core/stdio/bundle cell has an honest terminal evidence state and only passing cells support claims.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen only on architecture/scope contradiction; otherwise preserve failed/blocked/unsupported cells without cross-OS inference.

task_candidates.objective: Run separate real Codex installation, discovery, Skill routing, MCP launch, project scope, build A/B update, disable/uninstall, incompatible refusal, and CLI takeover cells on every claimed OS/scope.
verification: real supported Codex host install/use traces per OS/version/scope
cached/relative path launch without global CLI/node_modules/npx/scripts
same run/state survives update/disable/uninstall and CLI takeover
task_candidates.done_condition: Each claimed Codex host/OS/scope/lifecycle cell has raw real-host evidence; missing cells remain non-ready.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if Codex cannot launch the approved Node 20 bundled model or requires a conflicting policy/state authority; otherwise withhold only failing/missing claims.

task_candidates.objective: Run separate real Claude installation, root substitution, Skill routing, MCP launch, project scope, build A/B update/reload, disable/uninstall, incompatible refusal, and CLI takeover cells on every claimed OS/scope.
verification: real supported Claude host install/use traces per OS/version/scope
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT path substitution and documented reload behavior
same run/state survives update/disable/uninstall and CLI takeover
task_candidates.done_condition: Each claimed Claude host/OS/scope/lifecycle cell has raw real-host evidence; missing cells remain non-ready.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if Claude cannot launch the approved Node 20 bundled model or requires a conflicting policy/state authority; otherwise withhold only failing/missing claims.

task_candidates.objective: Run Codex and Claude side-by-side cross-host independence and compatible build takeover cells per OS, proving one host's lifecycle changes do not alter the other artifact/settings or shared Launchdeck state.
verification: same build identity but distinct host artifacts
disable/update/uninstall one host while other remains operational
task_candidates.done_condition: Cross-host evidence proves artifact/settings independence and shared-state/service lifetime only for exact passing OS cells.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen if host packages must mutate each other or own managed-task/shared-state lifetime; otherwise retain exact blocked/failed cells.

task_candidates.objective: Audit the final registry, schemas, Skill, manifests, packages, help, next actions, and evidence for leakage of every deferred/dropped operation/input/claim and for policy duplication.
verification: search and structured inventory of tools/capabilities/Skill/host manifests/next actions
no broad readiness field without evidence cell
task_candidates.done_condition: All exclusions are mechanically enforced and no generated or hand-authored surface implies deferred/dropped availability.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen instead of deleting an approved in-scope capability or activating any deferred capability to satisfy the audit.

task_candidates.objective: Finalize CLI/Skill/Plugin usage and compatibility documentation, package exports/help, evidence-indexed readiness labels, then run the complete regression, package, real-entrypoint, and claim audit.
verification: validate every readiness label against exact evidence cells
review diff for second authority/unsafe inputs/state lifecycle coupling
task_candidates.done_condition: All automated gates pass, documentation/help reflects actual supported surfaces, and readiness claims equal—not exceed—the evidence index; unavailable cells stay visible.
task_candidates.stop_reopen: Reopen on any architecture/scope contradiction, CLI compatibility break, unsafe public capability, Plugin-owned state/service lifetime, or unresolvable evidence overclaim.

integration_targets.use: Render candidates in phase order, preserving each temporary key as trace metadata while assigning final T### IDs; keep tests before their paired implementation tasks.
Index dependency edges, story/phase, write scopes, CAP/AC/MP/CA refs, and real-entrypoint evidence flags.
Carry complete-first scope, user-confirmed deferrals, no-rollup evidence rule, stop/reopen conditions, and exact coverage summaries.
Use expected_write_scope and dependencies to prevent overlapping writers; evidence-only tasks may not mutate production code.
Preserve US4 cells as exact host/version/OS/scope/scenario evidence and never translate missing/blocked cells into broad readiness.
